libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No environment or system properties are required. Meaningful tests can use the normal Java DSL by creating an in-process ActorSystem, running Source/Flow/Sink operations, awaiting CompletionStage results, and terminating the ActorSystem after assertions.
risks: Akka Streams tests are asynchronous; failing to await stream completion or terminate the ActorSystem can cause flaky or hanging tests.
Native Image verification remains authoritative because Akka loads reference.conf resources and uses actor/stream internals that may require generated reachability metadata.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Akka Streams is usable in-process with the target artifact alone; its required runtime dependencies are transitive and no Docker image, extra Maven dependency, environment variable, or system property is needed before meaningful coverage.
